Bombus_sandersoni.morphological_description: WARNING: Do NOT generate a large, bright bumble bee. B. sandersoni is a SMALL bee with a clean TWO-TONE pattern: yellow front half, SOLID BLACK rear half. Overall impression: a small, compact bee. The front (thorax + T1–T2) is yellow, the rear (T3–T5) is completely black with no markings. HEAD: face BLACK. Short round face. THORAX: yellow pile on dorsum with a BLACK spot or band between the wing bases. The yellow is warm but not bright lemon. ABDOMEN: T1 yellow. T2 yellow, may have some black hairs at the sides. T3–T5 ENTIRELY BLACK — solid black, no bands, no patches, no colour. This abrupt cutoff to solid black at T3 is the KEY diagnostic. There is NO pale or white band near the tail. HAIR: short and even — neat, not shaggy. SIZE: distinctly SMALL (worker 8–11 mm). This bee should look small relative to the flower it sits on. LEGS: corbiculae present (flat, shiny hind tibiae with hair fringe). Wings lightly tinted.

caste_options.worker: This is a WORKER (female, 8–11 mm). Very small bee. Clean TWO-TONE pattern: yellow front, solid black rear. Colour from front to back: yellow thorax (black between wings) → yellow T1 → yellow T2 (some black at sides) → SOLID BLACK T3 → SOLID BLACK T4 → SOLID BLACK T5. No bands on rear half. Corbiculae present (flat shiny hind tibiae). Short even hair.
caste_options.queen: This is a QUEEN (female, 15–17 mm). Same two-tone pattern as worker but noticeably larger. Yellow front, solid black rear. Thorax yellow with black between wings. T1–T2 yellow, T3–T5 SOLID BLACK. No pale band near tail. Corbiculae present.
caste_options.male: This is a MALE (9–12 mm). Yellow facial hair (unlike females). T1–T2 yellow, possibly more extensive yellow than females. T3+ mostly black. Antennae longer (13 segments). NO corbiculae — hind tibiae convex and hairy.

Bombus_ashtoni.morphological_description: WARNING: This is NOT a typical yellow-and-black bumble bee. Do NOT generate a bee with a bright yellow thorax. B. ashtoni is a PREDOMINANTLY BLACK bee with very limited pale markings. Overall impression: a large, robust, dark bumble bee. The body is mostly black with white near the tail end. HEAD: face and vertex entirely BLACK. No yellow on head. THORAX: sides mostly BLACK. A black spot or band between wing bases — this black band may be large, extending toward the abdomen. The thorax should appear mostly DARK. There may be a narrow pale collar at the front edge but the dominant impression is black. ABDOMEN: T1 BLACK. T2 BLACK. T3 mostly black with some yellow on the sides. T4–T5 WHITE — this white tail area is the single most important field mark. T6 BLACK. LEGS: NO CORBICULAE — hind tibiae convex and covered in dense hair (NOT flat and shiny). This is a cuckoo bee (social parasite). Medium length hair. Wings slightly darkened. Female 15–20 mm.

caste_options.female: This is a FEMALE (15–20 mm). CRITICAL: this bee is PREDOMINANTLY BLACK. Colour from front to back: BLACK face and vertex → mostly BLACK thorax (black sides, black between wings) → BLACK T1 → BLACK T2 → mostly black T3 (yellow on sides) → WHITE T4 → WHITE T5 → BLACK T6. The WHITE on T4–T5 is the key field mark — a pale tail on an otherwise very dark bee. NO CORBICULAE — hind legs convex and densely hairy. Medium length hair. Wings darkened.
caste_options.male: This is a MALE (13–16 mm). Similar to female but with light hairs on face and vertex. YELLOW instead of white hairs on the abdomen. Still predominantly dark overall. Antennae longer (13 segments). NO CORBICULAE — hind tibiae convex and densely hairy.

Bombus_flavidus.morphological_description: WARNING: Do NOT generate a bright lemon-yellow bumble bee, but also do NOT make this bee mostly dark. B. flavidus has a MOSTLY YELLOW thorax and a distinctive pattern on the abdomen. Overall impression: a small, scruffy cuckoo bee with more yellow than most — the thorax is MOSTLY YELLOW, not just a collar. HEAD: face BLACK with at most a few yellow hairs above antenna base. Vertex (top/back of head) YELLOW — this yellow vertex is a KEY DIAGNOSTIC for this species. THORAX: MOSTLY YELLOW with a black spot or occasionally a narrow black band between wing bases. The thorax should read as predominantly yellow with a dark centre mark. ABDOMEN: T1 YELLOW (may be black in western populations, yellow in eastern). T2 BLACK. T3 variable. T4 MOSTLY YELLOW with a small patch of black at front. T5–T6 BLACK. T6 shiny, strongly curled under abdomen and pointing anteriorly (Psithyrus diagnostic). The pattern front-to-back is: YELLOW vertex → YELLOW thorax → YELLOW T1 → BLACK T2 → variable T3 → MOSTLY YELLOW T4 (small black patch at front) → BLACK T5–T6. LEGS: NO CORBICULAE — hind tibiae convex and covered with hair. Small bee with medium length hair. Wings light brown. Female 17–18 mm.

caste_options.female: This is a FEMALE (17–18 mm). This bee has a MOSTLY YELLOW thorax — do NOT make it mostly dark. Colour from front to back: YELLOW vertex → MOSTLY YELLOW thorax (black spot between wings) → variable T1 → BLACK T2 → variable T3 → MOSTLY YELLOW T4 (small black patch at front) → BLACK T5–T6. Key features: (1) YELLOW vertex, (2) thorax MOSTLY YELLOW, (3) T2 BLACK, (4) T4 yellow/cream. T6 shiny. NO CORBICULAE — hind legs convex and densely hairy. Wings light brown.
caste_options.male: This is a MALE (11–15 mm). Face hair BLACK. T1–T3 sometimes nearly completely YELLOW. T4 YELLOW with at most a few black hairs near midline. T5 black. T6–T7 ORANGE, often with paler tips. Highly variable. Antennae medium length, flagellum 3x longer than scape. NO CORBICULAE — hind tibiae convex and covered with hair.
